$comment: FAMILY HUB PRE-CHECKS: which runtime conditions this product's calls need.
The harness owns WHEN checks run and what a failure means; this says WHICH.
Bindings, not code, for the same reason the safety rules are: the same
ApplianceControl plugin needs appliance_online:oven here and something else
entirely on another product. A probe id may carry a parameter after a colon,
so one probe serves every appliance.
Keep this HONEST rather than exhaustive. Every check is a way for the demo to
stall, and a check that guards nothing real is just a chance to be wrong: the
recipe box is a local file, so binding 'internet' to Recipes.FindRecipes would
invent a dependency that does not exist.
A binding naming a probe nobody implements FAILS at run time (see the engine) —
a precheck that quietly does nothing reads as a guarantee.
$comment_goal: true of ANY goal on this device — checked before the planner spends a token
$comment_capabilities: exact match and Module.* both apply — a module-wide rule is a floor, not a default
